methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional knowledge distribution infrastructure in Richmond, creating peer networks for AI fluency beyond just consuming platforms—a prototype for metro-anchored regional skill diffusion.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - news.vt.edu
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents grassroots capability building in a historically underserved region, testing whether deep skills can root outside traditional tech corridors when paired with place-based institutions.

signals.title: Economic development officials: Protect Virginia’s reputation and opportunity for responsible data center investment
signals.signal_reading: Southside officials pleading not to 'pull up the ladder' before they access data center economy reveals the infrastructure-as-prerequisite trap—regions waiting for concentrated capital investment rather than building distributed AI literacy independently.

signals.title: AI is here in Hampton Roads, and businesses and organizations are embracing it - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads businesses 'embracing' AI suggests platform-mediated adoption spreading to military-adjacent economy, but without indicators of deep technical capability—classic shallow distribution through turnkey tools rather than indigenous development capacity.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: AI trainer emphasizing workers must 'lead technology, not follow it' in Roanoke signals pedagogical orientation toward deep fluency rather than passive tool consumption—critical framing for whether New River Valley builds on Virginia Tech's technical foundation or defaults to vendor dependence.
